events.title: Boris Barnet, A Soviet Poet - Film Retrospective
events.description: A month-long retrospective dedicated to the boxer-turned-moviemaker and true wonder of intuition. Featuring films including The House on Trubnaya, Miss Mend, and more.

events.title: Klute (1971)
events.description: Jane Fonda and Donald Sutherland star in Alan J. Pakula's 1971 thriller. 114min / 35mm.

events.title: The House on Trubnaya (1927) with Live Piano
events.description: Boris Barnet's 1927 film with live piano accompaniment by Makia Matsumura. 91min / 35mm.

events.title: Battle Royale (2000)
events.description: Kenji Fukasaku's cult classic. 114min / DCP.

events.title: Miss Mend (1926)
events.description: Boris Barnet's 1926 film. Introduction by Metrograph Programmer Edo Choi. 270min / DCP.

events.title: Kill Bill: Vol. 1 (2003)
events.description: Quentin Tarantino's martial arts epic starring Uma Thurman. 111min / 35mm.

events.title: Kill Bill: Vol. 2 (2004)
events.description: The conclusion of Quentin Tarantino's revenge saga. 137min / 35mm.

events.title: The Bad and the Beautiful (1952)
events.description: Vincente Minnelli's Tinseltown tale starring Kirk Douglas and Lana Turner. 118min / 35mm.

events.title: Pompei: Below the Clouds
events.description: New release at IFC Center.

events.title: 2026 Oscar-Nominated Short Films
events.description: Annual showcase of Academy Award-nominated short films in Animated, Documentary, and Live Action categories.

events.title: Late Night Favorites: House (1977)
events.description: Nobuhiko Obayashi's cult horror classic. Part of Late Night Favorites series.

events.title: New York Philharmonic - Mirga Gražinytė-Tyla & Emanuel Ax
events.description: The NY Phil welcomes Mirga Gražinytė-Tyla and pianist Emanuel Ax for an evening of classical masterworks.

events.title: New York Philharmonic - Chamber Music Concert
events.description: Chamber music performance by NY Phil musicians.

events.title: New York Philharmonic - Gustavo Dudamel: Eroica & The People United
events.description: Gustavo Dudamel conducts Beethoven's Symphony No. 3 'Eroica'.

events.title: American Ballet Theatre - Othello
events.venue: David H. Koch Theater, Lincoln Center
events.description: Lar Lubovitch's gripping full-length ballet based on Shakespeare's tragic tale, set to Elliot Goldenthal's evocative score.

events.title: American Ballet Theatre - Mozartiana & Firebird
events.venue: David H. Koch Theater, Lincoln Center
events.description: George Balanchine's Mozartiana and Alexei Ratmansky's vibrant Firebird.

events.title: American Ballet Theatre - Raymonda Act III & Firebird
events.venue: David H. Koch Theater, Lincoln Center
events.description: Petipa's Raymonda Act III Grand Pas Hongrois paired with Ratmansky's Firebird.

events.title: American Ballet Theatre - Othello (Matinee)
events.venue: David H. Koch Theater, Lincoln Center
events.description: Matinee performance of Lar Lubovitch's Othello.

events.title: Wagner's Tristan und Isolde
events.description: New Production of Wagner's epic love story. March 9 - April 2, 2026.

events.title: Verdi's La Traviata
events.description: Verdi's beloved opera. March 20 - June 6, 2026.

events.title: Whitney Biennial 2026
events.description: The 82nd edition of the Whitney Biennial—the longest-running survey of contemporary American art—features 56 artists, duos, and collectives. Free for 25 and under. Opening coincides with Free Second Sunday.

events.title: Carol Bove
events.description: Surveying twenty-five years of Carol Bove's career, from her early drawings to ambitious new scrap metal and steel tubing 'collage sculptures' in the iconic rotunda.

events.title: Gabriele Münter: Contours of a World
events.description: Exhibition of works by German Expressionist painter Gabriele Münter.

events.title: Frida and Diego: The Last Dream
events.description: Exhibition of key works from MoMA's collection by Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera, presented in conjunction with the Metropolitan Opera's premiere of El Último Sueño de Frida y Diego.

events.title: Marcel Duchamp
events.description: First US retrospective of Marcel Duchamp's work since 1973, featuring approximately 300 works from 1900 to 1968.

events.title: Elizabeth Murray: Painters Progress
events.description: Exhibition of Elizabeth Murray's work. March 4 - May 25, 2026.

events.title: Gainsborough: The Fashion of Portraiture
events.description: The museum's first special exhibition dedicated to Thomas Gainsborough, exploring the relationship between his works and fashion. Through May 25, 2026.

events.title: Flora Yukhnovich's Four Seasons (Final Week)
events.description: Site-specific mural covering the walls of the museum's Cabinet, inspired by Fragonard's Four Seasons. Final week - closes March 9.

events.title: Live at the Frick: Isaac Mizrahi in Conversation with Aimee Ng
events.description: Fashion designer Isaac Mizrahi in conversation with Aimee Ng. SOLD OUT.

events.title: Raphael: Sublime Poetry (Preview Week)
events.description: First comprehensive exhibition on Raphael in the USA, featuring over 200 works. Opens March 29, 2026.

events.title: Uptown Rhythm Dance Festival - Donnetta Jackson / MADD Mixtape, Maria Torres
events.description: Featuring Donnetta Jackson / MADD Mixtape, Maria Torres, Nelida Tirado, Liliana Morales & Leah Banales. Co-curated by Alison Manning & Michelle Dorrance.

events.title: Uptown Rhythm Dance Festival - Chelsea Hoy, Fritzlyn Hector, Irene Koloseus
events.description: Featuring Chelsea Hoy, Fritzlyn Hector, Irene Koloseus, and Mai Lê Hô's LayeRhythm.

events.title: Ladies in the Shoe Performances
events.description: Tap performances conceived and directed by Dormeshia. Featuring Demi Remick, Josette Wiggin, Amanda Castro, Claudia Rahardjinato, Dormeshia.

events.title: Uptown Rhythm Dance Festival at Guggenheim - Kukai Dantza
events.description: Featuring Kukai Dantza: Nire Txokotik and Xianix Barrera: Quebrada at the Peter B. Lewis Theater.

events.title: Rotunda Dance Party: SCUFF Queer Country Dancing
events.description: SCUFF Queer Country Dancing in the Guggenheim Rotunda. Part of Uptown Rhythm Dance Festival.

events.title: JACK Quartet at 92NY
events.description: JACK Quartet returns with works developed through JACK Studio, featuring world premieres by Tristan Perich, Keir GoGwilt, Jules Reidy, and Ailie Ormston.

events.title: BAM Film Program
events.description: BAM's film program featuring independent, international, and repertory cinema.

events.title: Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man
events.description: Peaky Blinders film screening. Playing from March 6, 2026.

events.title: Film Forum Current Program
events.description: Independent and repertory cinema in the South Village.

events.title: Chamber Music Society - The Violin Today
events.description: Lecture on The Violin Today.

events.title: Jazz at Lincoln Center - Big Band Afrobeat to Afrobeats
events.description: Ulysses Owens Jr. with Michael Olatuja performing Big Band Afrobeat to Afrobeats.
